tool_summary: Concatenate and display file contents to stdout, or write contents to a target file via shell redirection.

rules.description: cat with output redirected to a file using >, which overwrites or creates the target file at its path.
rules.reversible_note: Reversible only if the target file existed before and was previously backed up; creating new files is inherently irreversible.

rules.description: cat with output appended to a file using >>, which adds content to an existing file or creates a new one.
rules.notes: Appending is reversible in the sense that old content is preserved; however, reversing requires manually stripping appended content.

default_rule.notes: Baseline behavior: reads file(s) and writes to stdout. May expose sensitive content if applied to private files — reviewer should consider privacy-breach risk in context.
